title: Fault Tolerant Quantum Computing

theory.introduction: Fault Tolerant Quantum Computing (FTQC) focuses on building reliable quantum computers that can perform long computations despite the presence of noise, decoherence, and operational errors. Quantum systems are extremely sensitive to environmental disturbances, making error correction essential for scalable quantum computation. Fault tolerance ensures that quantum computations remain accurate even when individual components fail.
key_points: Quantum systems are highly sensitive to noise
Fault tolerance allows reliable large-scale quantum computation
Quantum error correction protects quantum information
Logical qubits are encoded using multiple physical qubits

decoherence.description: Loss of quantum information due to environmental interaction

logical_qubits.description: Logical qubits are encoded using multiple physical qubits to protect information from errors.

quantum_error_correction.description: Quantum error correction (QEC) detects and corrects errors without directly measuring quantum states.
principles: encode logical qubit using entangled physical qubits

fault_tolerant_operations.description: Fault tolerant gates ensure that errors do not spread uncontrollably through quantum circuits.

fault_tolerance_threshold.description: Error rate below which quantum error correction can successfully maintain accurate computation.
fault_tolerance_threshold.importance: determines feasibility of large-scale quantum computers

challenges: large number of physical qubits required
open_questions: How many physical qubits are needed per logical qubit?
What is the best quantum error correction code?
Can fault tolerance be achieved with fewer qubits?
What hardware platform supports best error correction?
How close are current devices to fault tolerance threshold?
Can topological qubits reduce error correction cost?
summary: Fault Tolerant Quantum Computing enables reliable quantum computation despite noise and errors. By encoding logical qubits and applying quantum error correction techniques, quantum computers can perform long and complex computations accurately.
